lane: descent decode-path host-residency / graph-capturability + net step

inventory.trigger: every step (drafter proposes K=num_speculative_tokens width-1 tokens)
inventory.rationale: for index in range(token_count) is a STATIC Python loop (token_count=self.num_speculative_tokens, a fixed config int -> fixed trip count). Body is device .copy_ + model forward + get_top_tokens; NO .item()/.cpu(). Captured.

inventory.site: vLLM gpu_model_runner cudagraph (42 layers, M=32 int4-Marlin GEMM + star-attn); lawine #136 gemm_all_graphed=true
inventory.trigger: every step (verify the K drafted tokens)
inventory.rationale: the verify GEMMs are graph-captured (gemm_all_graphed); the eager star-attn launch idle is GPU-hidden behind per-layer GEMM (#136). This IS the anchor step.

inventory.trigger: every step (greedy token selection over the verify logits)
inventory.per_fire_cost_class: EAGER outside-graph memory-bound, 111.9 us/step (M=32 realistic); recoverable
inventory.rationale: runs EAGERLY outside the CUDA graph (serve_patch_pck04.py:17-20): it is not a host round-trip, but an outside-graph op that adds eager launch + [M,262144] BW work to the timed step. ANCHOR #1 (re-discovered).
inventory.greedy_safe_design: on the token-selection path replace scatter[M,262144]+LP+argmax_262144 with argmax(pruned[M,12288]) -> kept_ids remap (kept_ids ascending => first-occurrence tiebreak == full-vocab argmax; equivalence_rate=1.0). Keep full scatter+LP on the prompt_logprobs/PPL prefill path. Recovers ~111.9 us/step -> bar 4.862->4.808.

inventory.trigger: every step (greedy accept/reject prefix of the K drafted tokens)
inventory.rationale: the accept loop `for pos in range(num_draft_tokens)` is INSIDE the compiled Triton kernel -> on-device, no host round-trip. The wrapper's int(output_token_ids.shape[0]) reads a SHAPE (static), not a device data value. This is the CURRENT linear accept path; land #71's descent extends it but the accept-COMPARE primitive is already on-device.

inventory.site: land #71 build (descending accept-walk kernel); modeled in scripts/profiler/salvage_walk_overhead.py (lawine #147)
inventory.trigger: every step (resolve accepted spine length over the depth-9 tree)
inventory.rationale: lawine #147's SYNC-POINT lane: naive per-node bool(verify_argmax[u].eq(draft_tok).item()) is a host round-trip AND a capture break; the sync-free design (match-mask -> cumprod -> argmax-first-mismatch, device scalar) captures. CONSUMED as input -- not re-counted in this lane's residual.
inventory.greedy_safe_design: vLLM-v1 RejectionSampler (PR #14930 zero-sync): device match-mask/cumprod accept length; the next step's expand indexes by the device scalar (no .item()).

inventory.rationale: naive int(branch_scores.argmax().item()) is a host round-trip; device argmax + gather by device index avoids it. #147's lane (consumed).
inventory.greedy_safe_design: best=branch_scores.argmax(); gather the chosen branch by the DEVICE index.

inventory.rationale: naive n_accept=accept_len.item() is a host round-trip every step; keeping accept_len a DEVICE scalar (consumed by the next expand) avoids it. #147's lane (consumed).
inventory.greedy_safe_design: keep accept_len on device; next-step KV/context indexing uses the device scalar.

inventory.site: land #71 build (salvage-commit KV relocation over 37 served layers); chiku-inu trace tree-488-pw-fp32-v0 (board 20260614-111022-934); priced in scripts/profiler/salvage_kv_relocation_audit.py (my #157)
inventory.trigger: salvage steps (0.382/step); relocates accepted rows across 37 layers' K+V
inventory.per_fire_cost_class: host-loop 55.5 ms/step (host-bound) / vectorized 35.3 us/step / paged 20.3 us/step
inventory.rationale: ALL THREE host-residency classes if built as the naive host loop (per-layer x per-row D2H/H2D Python over 37 layers): a data-dependent Python loop CANNOT be CUDA-graph-captured -> pins the step host-bound (bar 32.59, descent 522->77 TPS, INFEASIBLE). ANCHOR #2 (re-discovered). Greedy-safe (bit-exact bf16 permutation, equivalence_rate=1.0).
inventory.greedy_safe_design: single FUSED device gather/scatter over the [L,W,H,D] stack by a DEVICE commit-index in one launch (index_select+index_copy_), OR a paged slot-map update (zero-copy). Device-index rule: the commit-index is produced ON-DEVICE by the accept walk and consumed without a host readout -> stays inside the captured graph. Vectorized: 35.3 us/step -> bar 4.880, descent 516 TPS.

inventory.trigger: every step (advance committed KV / block-table / slot_mapping by accept_len)
inventory.per_fire_cost_class: DEVICE copy (capturable) -- the slot-map form of the relocate
inventory.rationale: if accept_len is a device scalar (the #147 sync-free rule), the commit/advance is a device copy. This is exactly the relocate's zero-copy paged_slotmap variant (#157) + the #147 accept-length readout -- NOT a new op. Host-bound ONLY if accept_len is read to host (= the #147 accept_length_readout, already owned).
inventory.greedy_safe_design: advance slot-map / block-table by the DEVICE accept_len (no host readout).

inventory.site: land #71 build (rising depth-1 spine conditional); lawine #161 lane
inventory.rationale: the depth-1 spine conditional's added op-count is lawine #161's lane. Excluded here; a slot is armed (PR161_BOTH_BUGS_STEP_DELTA_PCT) to fold its both_bugs_step_delta_pct when it lands.

inventory.trigger: every step (hand accepted tokens to the CPU scheduler: KV mgmt / stop / streaming)
inventory.per_fire_cost_class: STRUCTURAL 1/step, ALREADY in the 1.2182 anchor; #147 marginal +0.52% (GPU-hidden)
inventory.rationale: exactly ONE host-sync per step is structurally unavoidable (every decode step streams); it is already in the 1.2182 anchor and GPU-hidden behind the GEMM tail. land does NOT fuse it.
inventory.greedy_safe_design: n/a (unavoidable; already priced in the anchor)

inventory.trigger: every step (write next-step drafter input from the accepted token)
inventory.rationale: device .copy_ of a device token inside the captured LOOPGRAPH body; no host round-trip.

host_loop_relocate.err: RuntimeError: Cannot copy between CPU and CUDA tensors during CUDA graph capture unless the CPU tensor is pinned. Please use tensor.pi
host_loop_relocate.description: #157 landmine: per-row .cpu()/.item() Python loop over layers

sync_bound_accept_walk.err:
AcceleratorError: CUDA error: operation failed due to a previous error during capture
Search for `cudaErrorStreamCaptureInvalidated' in ht

capturability_probe.note: EMPIRICAL capturability grounding of the taxonomy: device-resident relocate/accept-walk CAPTURE; host-loop relocate + per-node .item() walk BREAK capture. Distinct from #147 sync-COUNTING -- this measures graph-CAPTURABILITY.

net_step_budget.verdict: FITS: the FULL descent decode path fits inside step ~=1.2182 once all greedy-safe recoveries are applied. Realizable build (vectorized relocate + sync-free accept-walk + #154 stacked) nets 1.2086 units (bar 4.824); descent E[T]=5.04 clears at 522 TPS, both-bugs E[T]=5.207 at 540 TPS. The SOLE residual that blows the budget is a HOST-LOOP relocate (8.17 units, bar 32.59, descent->77 TPS) -- already classified (#157) with a greedy-safe vectorized design. descent_path_residual_host_ops_count=0: no UNCLASSIFIED host op hides on the path.

build_handoff.classification: FIELD SWEPT CLEAN -- the descent decode path host-residency surface is FULLY accounted: 2 anchors (#154 scatter+LP, #157 relocate), #147's consumed accept-walk sync surface, #161's excluded spine conditional, and the structural terminal sync. 0 NEW landmines.

Keep full scatter+LP on the prompt_logprobs/PPL prefill path. Recovers ~111.9 us/step -> bar 4.862->4.808.

feeds.fern_155_consolidator: net_clear_500_bar_descent_only=4.8241; the realizable build's operative bar (vectorized relocate + sync-free walk + #154).
feeds.lawine_161_spine_cost: both-bugs step slot ARMED (net_clear_500_bar_both_bugs=4.8241); fold #161 both_bugs_step_delta_pct when it lands.

build_handoff.launch_gate: INFORMS, does NOT authorize. The descent build is launch-de-risked on the host-residency axis IFF the relocate is vectorized/paged AND the accept-walk is sync-free. A host-loop relocate OR a sync-bound walk is the only way the step collapses, and both are already classified with greedy-safe designs.
